# BalconySolarHub

> Independent UK guide tracking the BS 7671 Amendment 4 transition for balcony / plug-in solar. Plain-English coverage of legality, the free G98 DNO notification, kit picks, savings at the current ~27p/kWh price cap, and the gap between "ratified" and "DIY-installable".

Updated 15 June 2026.

## Editorial principle

We do NOT publish "just plug it in, fully legal now" copy. The UK is in a real, documented transition window between BS 7671 Amendment 4 (in force 15 April 2026) and the expected BSI product standard for DIY plug-in kits (~July 2026, transitional period through ~October 2026). When we don't know something, we say so.

## Key pages

- [Is plug-in solar legal in the UK? (2026)](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/legal/): Flagship explainer of BS 7671 Amendment 4, what changed on 15 April 2026, what's still pending, the 800W cap, RCD and anti-islanding rules.
- [G98 DNO notification walkthrough](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/g98-notification/): Step-by-step UK DNO walkthrough — free, online, ~10 minutes. Covers UK Power Networks, SSEN, NGED, Northern Powergrid, Electricity North West, SP Energy Networks.
- [The 800W limit explained](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/guides/800w-limit/): Why the UK cap is 800W AC inverter output (not panel watts) and what happens above it.
- [Best UK balcony solar kits 2026](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/best-kits-2026/): Honest UK availability notes on EcoFlow STREAM, Anker SOLIX Solarbank 2, Zendure SolarFlow 800, APsystems EZ1-M, Hoymiles HMS-800.
- [UK savings calculator (£)](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/savings-calculator/): Free interactive calculator defaulting to the current UK energy price cap (~27p/kWh). Self-consumption model, honest assumptions.
- [Can you plug solar into a normal socket?](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/guides/normal-socket/): RCD, anti-islanding, outdoor-rated sockets, and why "just plug it in" isn't yet the legal default route.
- [Plug-in battery storage UK](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/guides/battery-storage/): Anker SOLIX Solarbank 2, Zendure SolarFlow 800, EcoFlow STREAM Ultra — how AC-coupled batteries fit the 800W rule.
- [Cost & payback (UK)](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/guides/cost-payback/): Real UK numbers — kit £200–£1,000, 600–900 kWh/year, ~£130–£200/year savings, 3–5 year payback.
- [UK balcony solar FAQ](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/faq/): Quick honest answers on legality, planning, leasehold, SEG, MCS, batteries.

## About

- [About & methodology](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/about/): Editorial principles, funding (Amazon UK Associates, no paid placement, no in-kind product).
- [Contact](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/contact/): hello@balconysolarhub.co.uk
- [Affiliate disclosure](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/affiliate-disclosure/)
- [Privacy](https://balconysolarhub.co.uk/privacy/)

## Verified UK facts

- BS 7671 Amendment 4: ratified 16 March 2026, in force 15 April 2026. Reclassifies ≤800W microgeneration as a portable appliance.
- Cap: 800W AC, single-phase, type-approved anti-islanding inverter, RCD-protected final circuit.
- G98 Connect & Notify: free online DNO notification, not an approval gate.
- BSI product standard for DIY plug-in kits: pending, expected ~July 2026; transitional period through ~October 2026.
- UK electricity price cap: ~27–28p/kWh (mid-2026). SEG generally not available for plug-in installs.
- Typical UK 800W kit: ~600–900 kWh/year, ~£130–£200 savings, 3–5 year payback for bare panels.

## License

Independent UK guidance. Free to read with attribution. Informational, not legal or electrical advice — confirm current rules with your DNO and a CPS-registered electrician before installing equipment.
